Box #1—Add a New Dimension
v1.01 (c) Deutsch & Associates, Inc Published by Marketing Rebel LLC
* How might you add movement (or stillness instead of movement)?
What moves in your business? Goods, people, customers, employees, money, information,
ideas? How might you start moving? How might you stop?
Idea Power by David L. Deutsch
MarketingRebelClub.com/marketing-rebel-courses/idea-power/
Idea Power / Idea Power Adventures / Adventure #1—Box 1 Add a New Dimension
Let’s apply this idea to your problem.
* How might you add something that can be seen, felt, heard or smelled
Remember to take it literally first. Then use a little free association. For example, you can
literally add sound elements like a sound played in your store or on your website. But what
does “hearing” make you think of? Word-of-mouth advertising? Listening?
Listening to customers?
In Adventure #1, you saw how changing or adding a dimension (as it relates
to the senses, movement or order) can generate an almost unlimited number of ideas.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 1 of 2 --

v1.01 (c) Deutsch & Associates, Inc Published by Marketing Rebel LLC
Idea Power by David L. Deutsch
MarketingRebelClub.com/marketing-rebel-courses/idea-power/
*Sometimes order is good, but sometimes it limits you. Sometimes, with randomness,
combinations occur, and unexpected things are possible that otherwise wouldn’t be.
Idea Power / Idea Power Adventures / Adventure #1—Box 1 Add a New Dimension
How might you replace order with randomness (or vice versa)?
*How might you look at things in a different way, or emphasize a
different dimension of the whole or any part?
Look at things not as you would look at them, but as someone else would look at them—
a customer or employee, for example, or someone totally unfamiliar with the situation.
*How might you add (or stop) change?
Where would adding it be helpful? Where would stopping it be helpful?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 2 --
